case_name: Singing River Health System v. Amy Brand, Individually and as Administratrix of the Estate of Debbie Edwards, and on Behalf of all Wrongful Death Beneficiaries of Debbie Edwards, and April Kattawar

summary.brief: Singing River Health System v. Amy Brand, Individually and as Administratrix of the Estate of Debbie Edwards, and on Behalf of all Wrongful Death Beneficiaries of Debbie Edwards, and April Kattawar. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Debbie Edwards was admitted to Singing River Hospital in Ocean Springs on May 27, 2018. She was suffering from weakness and passing bloody stool.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The next day, Debbie underwent a procedure called an esophagogastroduodenoscopy, or EGD, to diagnose and treat problems in her upper gastrointestinal tract.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Over the next few days, June 4 and 5, Debbie’s health began to fluctuate. After June 4, Dr. Stuart Phillips viewed her condition as improved; he moved her out of intensive care.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At 8:20 a.m., the hospital staff realized Singing River’s radiologist was on vacation. No one at Singing River could perform the critical operation to stop Debbie’s GI bleed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: With the patient now bleeding extensively, she was transferred back to ICU. A general surgical consultation was ordered to address the GI bleed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Even though efforts had been made hours before to transfer her to Ochsner, an ambulance was not called until 6:20 p.m., nearly five hours later.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The circuit court found the facility breached the standard of care it owed to the patient.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court found Brand incurred economic damages in the amount of $6,213.54 as well as non-economic damages of $375,000.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Debbie’s Estate also offered the expert testimony of Dr. Kyle Happel, a professor of critical care medicine.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Dr. Brown’s expert testimony persuaded the trial court that the weather conditions made flying too dangerous.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Brand testified that on June 5, when she got to the hospital, her mother was “very nauseous and . . . kept asking for Phenergan because that’s what helped her with the nause[a].”
evidence_index.speaker: Amy Brand

evidence_index.text: Kattawar testified that she arrived at Ochsner around 10:30 p.m. on June 5. Upon her arrival, her mother was “coding.”
evidence_index.speaker: April Kattawar

evidence_index.text: Competent expert testimony established this was a breach of the applicable standard of care and caused the damages awarded.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient admitted with weakness and bloody stool. Previous fundoplication procedure failed, resulting in a Mallory Weiss tear.
critical_findings: Mallory Weiss tear at failed fundoplication site

requestables.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD)
requestables.description: Procedure to diagnose and treat upper GI tract issues

reveal.result_summary: Mallory Weiss tear identified, bleeding not stopped
reveal.decision_impact: Prompted further EGDs and consideration of IR

requestables.name: Interventional Radiology Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation for potential embolization of bleeding artery

reveal.result_summary: IR not available due to radiologist on vacation
reveal.decision_impact: Contributed to delay in stopping bleed

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with weakness and bloody stool

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: EGD performed to identify source of bleeding

timeline_phases.name: Critical Decision Making
timeline_phases.description: Decision to transfer patient due to lack of IR capability

key_events.event: Decision to transfer to Ochsner Medical Center

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with ongoing GI bleed, IR not available
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing the patient's GI bleed?

options.description: Transfer patient immediately to a facility with IR capability
options.clinical_reasoning: Immediate transfer necessary due to lack of IR capability at current facility

options.description: Perform another EGD and delay transfer
options.clinical_reasoning: EGD unlikely to stop bleed, delay in transfer increases risk

actual_action_defendant.description: Performed another EGD and delayed transfer
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Attempted to manage bleed with EGD despite lack of IR

expected_action_court.description: Immediate transfer to facility with IR capability
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated immediate transfer was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in transfer led to prolonged bleeding and cardiac arrest
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate transfer to a facility with IR capability
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered cardiac arrest and died after prolonged bleeding

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to transfer patient promptly
Causation established - delay in transfer contributed to death

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Delay in transferring patient to facility with IR capability
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate transfer could have prevented death

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to Singing River Hospital
Decision to transfer to Ochsner Medical Center

court_findings.finding: Breach of standard of care by delaying transfer
